headline: Western Ethical Tradition: Aristotle, Kant, and Utilitarianism
description: The Western ethical tradition is dominated by three approaches: virtue ethics (Aristotle — character), deontology (Kant — duty), and consequentialism/utilitarianism (Bentham/Mill — outcomes). Contemporary ethics integrates elements of all three.

citation.name: Groundwork of the Metaphysics of Morals
Ethics: History, Theory, and Contemporary Issues (9th Edition, 2025)
citation.author: Cahn S.M., Markie P.
